description: The figure presents calculated isothermal phase diagrams of Fe-20Mn-Al-C alloys at 900°C (subfigure a) and 1200°C (subfigure b), depicting phase stability as a function of aluminum (0–20 wt%) and carbon (0–5 wt%) content at a fixed manganese level of 20 wt%. The diagrams delineate multiple phase fields including single-phase austenite (γ), ferrite (α), and various carbide phases such as κ-carbide (κ), cementite (M₃C), aluminum carbide (Al₄C₃), and graphite (gra). Subfigure (a) shows the solid-state equilibrium at 900°C with distinct regions for γ+κ, α+κ, and α+γ duplex structures, while subfigure (b) illustrates the high-temperature configuration at 1200°C introducing liquid (L) phase regions and expanded austenite stability. Experimental data points from various studies are superimposed as symbols (circles, triangles, squares) representing observed phase combinations, validating the thermodynamic calculations. These diagrams serve as fundamental references for understanding precipitation behavior and phase transformations in lightweight austenitic steels.
